name: HoomenX-inspired design system
description: High-level creative direction distilled from the reference: extreme minimalist dark editorial UI, typography-led hierarchy, pill geometry, and generous negative space. Use this file to replicate the same mood, structure, and component language across the Single word app.

design_principles.guidance: Every element must earn its place. No ornamental chrome, no gratuitous gradients on UI chrome. Let black (or void) space do the work.
Primary surfaces are near-black or true black; primary text is white or off-white. Muted text is cool gray, never mid-gray on mid-gray.
Pair a bold editorial serif for brand and hero moments with a neutral geometric sans for everything functional. Weight and size do the hierarchy, not boxes.
Interactive controls (buttons, fields) use fully rounded ends—pill shapes—not soft rectangles. Keeps the UI feeling contemporary and calm.
Large outer margins, airy line-height for body copy, and clear separation between regions (nav vs hero vs footer). Avoid dense stacking.
Combine left-aligned reading blocks with right-aligned utility clusters (nav, secondary actions) to create tension and balance without clutter.
In RSVP / single-word reading views, the optical center letter (ORP) stays locked; only its accent colour may change—never the global type colour for the word body.

background.description: True pitch black for main canvas and premium feel.

background_alt.description: Optional step for subtle depth (e.g. footer band) without breaking black-field minimalism.

text.description: Primary is pure white for headlines and critical labels; secondary for supporting sentences; muted for placeholders, hints, and tertiary links.

border.description: Thin hairline strokes on dark. Prefer semi-transparent white over solid gray for elegance.

primary_on_dark.description: Primary CTA: solid white fill, black text—inverts the page.

secondary_on_dark.description: Secondary CTA: outline pill, white label, no fill.

starfield_background.description: Replace flat #000 with a deep space + subtle stars layer behind content; UI panels and the word row should feel blended (no heavy cards).
starfield_background.pivot_accent: user-chosen only; default indigo-violet family acceptable (e.g. #A5B4FC) against dark void

brand_serif.character: High-contrast serif; modern slab or bold Didone feel—authoritative, fashion/editorial.

ui_sans.role: Body, navigation, labels, buttons, form text
ui_sans.character: Clean geometric sans; neutral, highly legible on black.

rules: Use serif sparingly—brand and hero only unless the screen is purely editorial.
Default UI chrome (nav, forms, reader controls) stays sans.
Do not mix more than two type families on one screen.
Reader word display: bold sans or controlled serif only if explicitly part of brand; size driven by slider, line-height tight (≈1) for ORP alignment.

principles: Prefer fewer, larger gaps over many tight ones.
Hero and brand areas need the largest breathing room.
Keep form groups visually grouped; separate unrelated sections with dividers or space—not boxes.

radii_and_shape.rule: All primary buttons and text inputs use full pill geometry. Small icon-only controls may use perfect circles.
radii_and_shape.cards: Avoid heavy cards on black; if needed, use hairline border + transparent fill, large radius (16px) at most—not default for this system.

elevation_and_surface.default: Flat. Depth comes from contrast and space, not drop shadows on chrome.
elevation_and_surface.exceptions: Primary filled button may use a very soft shadow for lift; starfield reader may use minimal glow on focal letter only, not on panels.

dividers.style: 1px linear gradient: transparent → line → transparent (hairline, low opacity white).
dividers.usage: Separate major sections without adding visual weight.

navigation_header.structure: Logo or wordmark left; primary nav links right; optional tertiary link (e.g. presentation) as uppercase muted label.
navigation_header.link_style: No underline by default; hover lightens color to primary white
navigation_header.spacing: Generous horizontal gap between nav items (e.g. 24–40px)

states.hover: Slightly off-white background (#F5F5F5) or subtle lift via shadow

button_secondary.optional_icon: Small inline icon (e.g. download) before or after label, same stroke weight as border

text_input.focus: Thin light ring or border brightening—no thick outlines

textarea.shape: large radius (12–16px) if full pill is awkward; still soft, not sharp corners

file_input.treatment: Match textarea border language; muted label copy

footer.layout: Two or three columns on large screens; stacked on small
footer.content: Brand line, legal links, short utility line (e.g. keyboard hints)
footer.alignment: space-between or split left/right for balance

icon_button_utility.description: Subdued; not competing with primary CTAs

reader_word_block.container: No card; word sits on void or starfield
reader_word_block.guide_lines: Hairline horizontal rules equidistant above/below word band; centered on word row only (exclude hints)
reader_word_block.pivot: Only pivot uses accent colour; flanking segments use fixed white/off-white
reader_word_block.alignment: Three-column grid: equal side tracks, pivot column auto—keeps ORP on screen center

landing.hero: Eyebrow (accent colour allowed) → headline → lead → primary + secondary CTAs
landing.feature_grid: Numbered labels (01, 02) in muted caps; short titles; muted body

reader_app.order: Paste/upload first, then reading surface, then controls

motion.principle: Restrained. Short fades and 150–200ms ease on hover; no playful bounces on core reading UI.

content_voice.tone: Calm, direct, premium—short labels, no exclamation clutter.

ai_implementation_notes: Map semantic tokens (background.primary, text.muted) in CSS :root; avoid hardcoding one-off hex except in this design.json.
When adding components, ask: would this survive on a black field with only type and a hairline? If not, simplify.
Reader view: never reintroduce global font colour pickers or background pickers; starfield + fixed light word colour + pivot accent only.
Use clamp() for responsive type and spacing to preserve editorial air on all viewports.
Reference image asset (if bundled): preserve HoomenX-like hierarchy: serif brand bottom-left energy, sans utility everywhere else.
